message: Billing provider NPI is missing from the trading partner profile.
whyItMatters: Availity requires a valid type-2 (group) NPI on every 837P claim. Without it, every transmission rejects with 277CA category A7.
resolution: Enter the billing provider NPI on the Trading Partner settings page.

message: Billing provider NPI is present but fails the Luhn checksum.
whyItMatters: Availity validates the NPI checksum before transmitting; an invalid NPI is rejected at the clearinghouse rather than the payer, costing a full re-submission cycle.
resolution: Double-check the 10-digit NPI on the NPPES registry and correct it on the Trading Partner settings page.

message: Billing tax ID / EIN is missing from the trading partner profile.
whyItMatters: Availity's trading partner agreement and the 837P REF*EI segment both require the practice EIN. Without it, enrollments and claims cannot be processed.
resolution: Enter the 9-digit EIN on the Trading Partner settings page.

message: Billing tax ID / EIN must be exactly 9 digits.
whyItMatters: A malformed EIN will be rejected at the clearinghouse 277CA and prevent the claim from ever reaching the payer.
resolution: Correct the EIN to 9 numeric digits on the Trading Partner settings page.

whyItMatters: The 837P pay-to address (Loop 2010AB / N3-N4) is a required HIPAA segment. A missing line 1, city, state, or ZIP causes a hard 277CA rejection.
resolution: Enter the full pay-to street, city, state, and ZIP on the Trading Partner settings page.

message: No authorized representative listed for the Availity trading partner agreement.
whyItMatters: Availity requires a named authorized representative on the TPA. While claims still transmit, enrollment changes and password resets are blocked until a representative is on file.
resolution: Enter the authorized representative's name, email, and phone on the Trading Partner settings page.
